# Seychelles city guide

## Overview

The Seychelles is an archipelago of 115 islands in the Indian Ocean, home to some of the most photographed beaches on earth. Giant granite boulders frame turquoise water, giant tortoises roam freely, and the pace of life is beautifully slow. Mahe is the main island, while La Digue is the car-free paradise where bikes rule.

**Best time to visit**: Apr-May and Oct-Nov for calm seas between the trade wind seasons. Mar-Apr and Oct-Nov are best for diving visibility.
**Currency**: Seychellois Rupee (SCR). 14 SCR ≈ 1 USD. Euros and USD also widely accepted.
**Language tip**: Creole, English, and French are all official. Everyone speaks English. "Bonzour" (hello) in Creole is a warm opener.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Victoria (Mahe) | Capital, compact | Market, Hindu temple, clock tower, supplies | Bus from anywhere on Mahe |
| Beau Vallon (Mahe) | Beach, touristy | Best public beach on Mahe, water sports, restaurants | Bus from Victoria (20 min) |
| Anse Royale (Mahe) | Quiet, scenic | Snorkeling, local restaurants, calm waters | Bus from Victoria (30 min) |
| Anse Source d'Argent (La Digue) | Iconic, stunning | The world's most photographed beach, granite boulders | Bike from ferry port (15 min) |
| La Passe (La Digue) | Village, charming | Ferry port, bike rentals, restaurants, local life | Ferry from Mahe/Praslin |
| Grand Anse (La Digue) | Wild, beautiful | Dramatic surf beach, hiking, fewer tourists | Bike from La Passe (20 min) |
| Praslin | Lush, resort | Vallee de Mai, Anse Lazio beach, luxury resorts | Ferry from Mahe (1 hr) or flight (15 min) |
| Silhouette Island | Remote, pristine | Eco-luxury, hiking, untouched nature | Boat from Mahe (45 min) |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Marie Antoinette Restaurant | Creole | Victoria (Mahe) | $$ | Colonial plantation house serving traditional Creole set menu. The same family recipe for decades. | Creole set menu (fish, chicken, rice, chutney) |
| La Plage | Beach restaurant | Beau Vallon (Mahe) | $$ | Feet-in-the-sand dining with fresh grilled fish. The catch of the day with garlic butter is simple perfection. | Grilled red snapper, octopus salad |
| Chez Jules | Creole | La Passe (La Digue) | $$ | Family-run Creole restaurant near the ferry. Fresh curry, grilled fish, and the warmest service. | Fish curry, grilled octopus, papaya salad |
| Boat House | Seafood | Beau Vallon (Mahe) | $$ | Beachfront restaurant with live music some evenings. Seafood platter to share while watching the sunset. | Seafood platter, Seybrew beer |
| Les Lauriers Eco Hotel Restaurant | Creole | La Passe (La Digue) | $$ | Hotel restaurant that's open to all. Traditional Creole buffet nights are generous and authentic. | Creole buffet, bat curry (seasonal) |
| Beau Vallon Night Market | Street food | Beau Vallon (Mahe) | $ | Wednesday and Friday evenings. Grilled fish, Creole curries, tropical fruit, and local music. | Grilled fish, breadfruit chips, coconut nougat |
| Cafe de l'Horloge | Cafe | Victoria (Mahe) | $ | Simple cafe near the clock tower. Good coffee, Creole pastries, and people-watching. | Coffee, coconut tart, samosa |
| Maison Marengo | Creole | Anse Reunion (La Digue) | $$ | Charming garden restaurant with excellent Creole dishes. Friendly owners, relaxed pace. | Fish curry, octopus in coconut milk |
| La Grande Maison | Fine dining | Praslin | $$$ | Upscale Creole-French fusion on Praslin. Beautiful setting in a restored colonial house. | Tasting menu, grilled lobster |
| Takamaka Rum Distillery | Bar/tour | Mahe | $$ | Seychelles' own rum distillery. Tour the facility, taste the range, buy bottles to take home. | Rum tasting flight, coconut rum |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Anse Source d'Argent | Beach | La Digue | $ | 3-4 hours | Giant granite boulders, shallow turquoise water, white sand. The most photographed beach in the world. Entry through L'Union Estate. |
| Vallee de Mai | Nature | Praslin | $$ | 2-3 hours | UNESCO World Heritage forest home to the Coco de Mer palm with the world's largest seed. Primeval atmosphere. |
| Morne Seychellois National Park | Nature | Mahe | Free | 3-5 hours | Hiking trails through rainforest to the highest point on Mahe. The Copolia Trail has panoramic views. |
| La Digue Bike Tour | Adventure | La Digue | $ | Full day | Rent a bike and explore the entire island. No cars, just bikes, beaches, and granite boulders around every bend. |
| Snorkeling at Anse Lazio | Nature | Praslin | Free | 2-3 hours | Consistently ranked among the world's best beaches. Excellent snorkeling right from shore. |
| Giant Tortoise Sanctuary | Nature | La Digue/Curieuse | $-$$ | 1-2 hours | Meet Aldabra giant tortoises up close. Curieuse Island has them roaming wild. |
| Victoria Market (Sir Selwyn Selwyn-Clarke Market) | Culture | Victoria (Mahe) | Free | 1 hour | Small but vibrant. Fish, spices, tropical fruit, and local crafts. Best on Saturday mornings. |
| Sainte Anne Marine National Park | Nature | Off Mahe | $$ | Half day | Glass-bottom boat or snorkeling tour through protected marine park. Sea turtles, reef fish, coral. |
| L'Union Estate | Culture | La Digue | $ | 1-2 hours | Coconut plantation, vanilla farm, giant tortoises, and the gateway to Anse Source d'Argent. |
| Sunset at Beau Vallon | Scenic | Mahe | Free | 1 hour | The best sunset spot on Mahe. Walk the beach, watch the sky turn orange, join the night market if timing is right. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Beau Vallon Beach Bars | Bars | Beau Vallon (Mahe) | $$ | Low-key beachfront bars with cocktails and sometimes live Sega music. |
| Tequila Boom | Bar/club | Victoria (Mahe) | $$ | The closest thing to a nightclub in Seychelles. Local crowd, Sega and pop music, weekends only. |
| Barrel Bar | Bar | Victoria (Mahe) | $$ | Small craft cocktail bar popular with expats and locals. Good rum selection. |
| La Digue Lodge Bar | Bar | La Digue | $$ | Relaxed evening drinks by the beach. Guitar music some nights. Island pace. |
| Resort Bars | Hotel bars | Multiple | $$-$$$ | Most resorts have sunset cocktail bars. Four Seasons and Raffles on Praslin are exceptional. |

## Transit tips

- **Mahe buses**: Public bus system covers the main island. Cheap but schedules can be irregular. Runs roughly 6 AM to 7 PM.
- **La Digue bikes**: The island has almost no cars. Rent a bike at the ferry port (about $10/day). The only way to explore.
- **Inter-island ferries**: Cat Cocos ferry connects Mahe, Praslin, and La Digue. Book ahead in peak season. 1 hour to Praslin.
- **Flights**: Air Seychelles flies Mahe to Praslin (15 min). Worth it if you're short on time.
- **Taxis on Mahe**: Available but not metered. Agree on price first. Can hire for half-day island tours.
- **Car rental on Mahe**: Left-hand drive, mountainous roads, beautiful scenery. Book ahead during peak season.
- **Pro tip**: La Digue is best explored by bike. Rent one immediately upon arrival at the ferry port.

## Useful Creole phrases

| English | Seychellois Creole | Romanization |
|---|---|---|
| Hello | Bonzour | bohn-ZOOR |
| How are you? | Ki dir? | kee deer |
| Thank you | Mersi | mare-SEE |
| Very good | Byen bon | bee-yen BOHN |
| Goodbye | Orwar | or-WAHR |
| Please | Silvouple | seel-voo-PLEH |
| Cheers! | Sante! | sahn-TAY |
| Beautiful | Zoli | ZOH-lee |
